mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in industrial production signals increasing real economic output, which drives higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows negative month-over-month growth in at least 40% of periods while SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index indicate a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and erodes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increase month-over-month
failure_condition: In months when both series rise, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, a decline in housing starts signals a deepening housing recession that amplifies negative economic spillovers, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% and HOUST declines month-over-month, SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales indicate strong consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: When RSAFS grows month-over-month, SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect robust labor demand and income growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200,000 in a single month
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises more than 200K, SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 30% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous upticks in industrial production and retail sales indicate synchronized expansion in both supply and demand sides of the economy, reinforcing each other to produce sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: In months when both series increase, SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When both unemployment and inflation are elevated (stagflation), monetary policy faces a credibility trade-off and fiscal support is limited, creating headwinds for equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances.
